rulings.Case Title: W. WONG CONSTRUCTION CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPO VS. WATT KIN YUEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn; Mtn For Leave To File 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 10, 2007, LINE 1. PLAINTIFF JAIDIN CONSULTING GROUP, L.L.C.'S MOTION For Leave To File FIRST Amended Complaint IS GRANTED BASED ON THE POLICY OF ALLOWING LIBERAL AMENDMENTS. IF THE WATT PARTIES SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFY WHAT DISCOVERY IS NECESSARY AS A CONSEQUENCE OF THE MOTION, THE COURT WILL CONSIDER CONTINUING THE AMENDMENT ON THE PAYMENT OF THE REASONABLE COST OF NECESSARY DISCOVERY. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 10, 2007, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF LUMBERMENS MUTUAL CASUALTY COMPANY'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To C.C.P. SECTION 664.6 IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 1, 2007 PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHERYL MORONG VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 10, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT R.C.A. CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON SCHEIDING AND ANDREWS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DESCRIBE THE ESSENTIAL FACTS AND TO EXPLAIN THE DELAY FROM THE TIME THE CASE WAS FILED UNTIL JULY 30, 2007 IN SEEKING THIS DISCOVERY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SHARYAR AZIZ JR. VS. RICHARD AICARDI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Richard Aicardi, Michael Aicardi And James Aicardi'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 10, 2007, LINE 16. DEFENDANTS RICHARD AICARDI, MICHAEL AICARDI AND JAMES AICARDI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. rulings.Case Title: ELBA GUTIERREZ/SAENZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Hearing For The San Francisco City Attonrey'S Office To Show Cause Why The Administrative Record Has Not Been Completed
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 10, 2007, LINE 13. PETITONER ELBA GUTIERREZ-SAENZ'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING AS TO WHY THE CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD HAS NOT BEEN COMPLETED: HEARING REQUIRED. PARTIES ARE TO ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF WHEN THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD WILL BE COMPLETED AND WHEN THE WRIT WILL BE HEARD. DID THE A.L.J. MAKE ANY FINDINGS AS TO THE LOCATION OF PETITIONER'S PRINCIPAL PLACE OF RESIDENCE? HOW MANY CHILDREN DOES THE PETITIONER HAVE, WHAT ARE THEIR AGES AND OCCUPATIONS, WHERE DO THEY RESIDE? WHAT IS THE PETITIONER'S ANNUAL INCOME? WHAT ASSETS DOES SHE HAVE? =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL V DISCEPOLA VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Improper Declarations
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 10, 2007, LINE 14. RESPONDENT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Strike Improper Declarations IS GRANTED. THE DECISION BELOW IS SUBJECT TO REVIEW UNDER ORDINARY MANDAMUS (C.C.P. SECTION 1085). ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS APPLIES WHERE THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION WAS MADE AFTER A HEARING REQUIRED BY LAW. THE STEP TWO PROCEEDING IS NOT SUCH A HEARING. OHTON V BOARD OF TRUSTEES, ETC. (2007) 148 CAL. APP. 4TH 749, 766. THE RECORD MAY NOT BE SUPPLEMENTED. BURNETT V THE REGENTS, ETC. (1995) 35 CAL. APP. 4TH 843, 853. =(302/PJM/AY)
